Reviews Contact

Elevate Your Boston Bathroom

Transform your historic home with a bath remodel that honors its character. Serving Cambridge, Newton, Brookline, and Quincy with craftsmanship built for New England winters.

Call Now: 555-bat-r91

Get a FREE Estimate Today!

Rooted in Boston, Built for You

Founded in 2012, Summit Bath Remodel began with a simple mission: to bring thoughtful design and durable craftsmanship to Boston's distinctive homes. Our team grew up understanding the quirks of triple-deckers and brownstones—and how to remodel them right.

Learn More About Us
Since 2014 Established
Locally Owned

Our Bath Remodel Services

Professional bath remodel solutions tailored to your needs.

Full Bathroom Renovation

Complete transformations for Boston's historic homes—from structural updates to final finishes.

Learn More →

Walk-In Shower Conversion

Safe, accessible showers designed for Boston's narrow bathrooms.

Learn More →

Period-Appropriate Tile Design

Authentic tile selections that honor your home's era.

Learn More →

Radiant Floor Heating

Warmth underfoot for chilly New England mornings.

Learn More →

Brownstone Bathroom Restoration

Sensitive updates for Back Bay and South End landmarks.

Learn More →

Frequently Asked Questions

Get answers to the most common questions about our bath remodel services.

How do you handle uneven floors in old homes?

We use laser leveling and custom shimming techniques, often installing a mortar bed under tile that can be adjusted to the home's natural slope.

Can you match my historic tile?

Our local suppliers carry reproduction patterns from the 1920s-50s, and we've successfully sourced original salvage tile from Boston-area architectural reclaimers.

Reviews From Our Bath Remodel Customers

See what our satisfied customers have to say about their experience.

"Summit transformed our 1890s South End bathroom without losing any original character. They found matching marble for the damaged wainscoting and designed a custom vanity that fits our curved wall perfectly. The radiant heat makes winter mornings bearable!"

Eleanor W. Brownstone Bathroom Restoration

"Our Newton Colonial had a tiny 1950s bathroom stuck in time. Summit's team reconfigured the layout to include a spacious walk-in shower without moving any plumbing stacks. The penny tile they sourced looks like it's always been there."

Marcus T. Walk-In Shower Conversion

"Finding someone who understood our Cambridge Victorian's aesthetic was crucial. They reproduced our damaged border tile by working with a local ceramicist and installed it with attention to historic detail. Five years later, not a single crack despite Boston's temperature swings."

Priya K. Period-Appropriate Tile Design
